topic: Contributor Network to share your cheatsheets

messages.content:
SAIR Contributor Network is now live.

We’ve officially launched the SAIR Contributor Network, a new space for the community to share cheatsheets, exchange ideas, and learn from each other across the SAIR competition platform.
https://competition.sair.foundation/contributor-network

If you’ve developed a useful cheatsheet, found a promising strategy, or discovered something others may benefit from, we encourage you to share it with the community. Our hope is that the SAIR Contributor Network will help strong ideas spread faster and make collaboration easier across the platform.

Next step: if you share a cheatsheet, we will benchmark it and display the results on the leaderboard. This feature will be available soon.

We are also considering how adopted community-shared cheatsheets should be reflected in the final evaluation policy. We will publish the relevant rules once that part of the policy is finalized.

Video tutorial:
https://youtu.be/0pTLiD-2qjQ

If you have any questions or thoughts while using it, you are very welcome to discuss them in this topic.

messages.content:
Would it be possible to be able to select multiple referenced users or sheets for the reference graph? Currently limited to a single reference, iv added in Description: the additional information and references >>

Description

//Spine-Shatter Logic: Adam Betka EQT01-000015
//3-Element Divergence (G9): Reza Jamei EQT01-000021

messages.subject: Contributor Network to share your cheatsheets
Contributor Network to share your cheatsheets

messages.content:
Dear community, we have posted a cheatsheet into community contribution, and we have got wildly different results from our internal models (w/ parameters aligned to official rules). We are in particular concerned with the level of misalignment on the output of the individual problems, that is up to 35% of the overall answers - is anyone experiencing the same issues?

here more details:

Cross-Dataset Summary

Dataset SAIR Acc Our Acc SAIR Macro F1 Our Macro F1 Mismatches
hard2 46.5% 48.5% 0.428 0.467 56/200 (28%)
hard3 60.8% 58.0% 0.585 0.573 143/400 (36%)

Hard 2 (200 problems)

SAIR Ours (local)
Accuracy 46.5% (93/200) 48.5% (97/200)
TP 21 30
FP 28 33
FN 79 70
TN 72 67
TRUE Precision 0.429 0.476
TRUE Recall 0.210 0.300
TRUE F1 0.282 0.368
FALSE Precision 0.477 0.489
FALSE Recall 0.720 0.670
FALSE F1 0.574 0.565
Macro F1 0.428 0.467

Hard 3 (400 problems)

SAIR Ours (local)
Accuracy 60.8% (243/400) 58.0% (232/400)
TP 75 90
FP 37 63
FN 120 105
TN 168 142
TRUE Precision 0.670 0.588
TRUE Recall 0.385 0.462
TRUE F1 0.489 0.517
FALSE Precision 0.583 0.575
FALSE Recall 0.820 0.693
FALSE F1 0.682 0.628
Macro F1 0.585 0.573
messages.subject: Contributor Network to share your cheatsheets

edit_history.prev_content:
Dear community, we have posted a cheatsheet into community contribution, and we have got wildly different results from  our internal models (w/ parameters aligned to official rules). We are in particular concerned with the level of misalignment on the output of the individual problems, that is up to 35% of the overall answers - is anyone experiencing the same issues?

here more details:

## Cross-Dataset Summary

| Dataset | SAIR Acc | Our Acc | SAIR Macro F1 | Our Macro F1 | Mismatches |
|---------|----------|---------|---------------|-------------|------------|
| hard2   | 46.5%    | 48.5%   | 0.428         | 0.467       | 56/200 (28%) |
| hard3   | 60.8%    | 58.0%   | 0.585         | 0.573       | 143/400 (36%) |

## Hard 2 (200 problems)

```markdown
|                | SAIR       | Ours (local) |
|----------------|------------|--------------|
| **Accuracy**   | 46.5% (93/200) | 48.5% (97/200) |
| TP             | 21         | 30           |
| FP             | 28         | 33           |
| FN             | 79         | 70           |
| TN             | 72         | 67           |
| TRUE Precision | 0.429      | 0.476        |
| TRUE Recall    | 0.210      | 0.300        |
| **TRUE F1**    | 0.282      | 0.368        |
| FALSE Precision| 0.477      | 0.489        |
| FALSE Recall   | 0.720      | 0.670        |
| **FALSE F1**   | 0.574      | 0.565        |
| **Macro F1**   | **0.428**  | **0.467**    |
```

## Hard 3 (400 problems)

```markdown

|                | SAIR       | Ours (local) |
|----------------|------------|--------------|
| **Accuracy**   | 60.8% (243/400) | 58.0% (232/400) |
| TP             | 75         | 90           |
| FP             | 37         | 63           |
| FN             | 120        | 105          |
| TN             | 168        | 142          |
| TRUE Precision | 0.670      | 0.588        |
| TRUE Recall    | 0.385      | 0.462        |
| **TRUE F1**    | 0.489      | 0.517        |
| FALSE Precision| 0.583      | 0.575        |
| FALSE Recall   | 0.820      | 0.693        |
| **FALSE F1**   | 0.682      | 0.628        |
| **Macro F1**   | **0.585**  | **0.573**    |
```

messages.content: @Erzio I just DMed you to check the details for the issue. Thanks!
messages.subject: Contributor Network to share your cheatsheets

messages.content: Is there an estimated deadline for when a shared sheet would finish being benchmarked before April 20th? For example, if something is submitted by April 19th at 12:00 PM, would it be tested by April 19th at 11:30 PM? I’m looking for an idea of a safe submission deadline if I want to review the benchmarking results before the April 20th, 23:59 AoE cutoff. Additionally, are you monitoring submission locations or I should assume the deadline is 23:59 UTC regardless of location.
messages.subject: Contributor Network to share your cheatsheets
